articles.title: The Strategic Multi-Layered Predicament of the Iran War and the Requirements for a Way Out
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: In 2026, a US-Israeli campaign against Iran escalated into a multidimensional impasse. Despite heavy strikes, Iran survives, retains leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, and carries out asymmetric responses. Ceasefire, blockade, and talks in Islamabad have failed to yield a victory narrative for either side.

articles.title: Stalemate: A Fragile Balance in the Iran War
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: US efforts to impose demands on Iran by force have failed, while Iran’s attempt to enforce its own terms through selective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has also fallen short. The result is a tense stalemate that could still shift toward renewed escalation or a negotiated settlement.
